description: Pin the devcontainer's corepack pnpm install to the version declared in package.json's packageManager field and add an automated drift guard so the two sources cannot silently diverge.

userStories.title: Pin the Dockerfile pnpm version
userStories.description: As the harness developer, I want the devcontainer to install the exact pnpm version the repo declares so that container builds are reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: The .devcontainer/Dockerfile corepack line reads `corepack prepare pnpm@10.33.0 --activate` (exact version from package.json packageManager)
`grep -c 'pnpm@latest' .devcontainer/Dockerfile` returns 0
Exactly one line changed; `git diff --stat` shows only the corepack line in the Dockerfile
`.devcontainer/Dockerfile` is a protected path (protected-paths.txt:43); change is additive hardening (one-line pin), not deletion — override note is documented in prd.md
Hadolint passes on the Dockerfile (hadolint exit 0); any new warning is fixed or added to .hadolint.yaml with rationale

userStories.title: Add the pnpm-pin drift-guard script
userStories.description: As the harness developer, I want a script that detects pnpm-version drift between the Dockerfile and package.json so the invariant is machine-checkable.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/check-pnpm-pin.sh exists with shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, is executable, and uses no jq (grep/sed/awk only)

Accepts optional `--dockerfile <path>` and `--package-json <path>` args, defaulting to repo-root-relative paths resolved from `$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)` so it is CWD-independent and test-injectable
Extracts the pnpm version from package.json packageManager, normalizing away any `+sha…` integrity suffix, and from the Dockerfile `corepack prepare pnpm@<ver>` line
Exits 0 when the two normalized versions match
On mismatch exits non-zero with the exact single line: `pnpm pin drift: Dockerfile pins pnpm@<X>, package.json declares pnpm@<Y> — update .devcontainer/Dockerfile to match`
Exits non-zero with a distinct message if a source is unreadable, the corepack pin line is missing, or the Dockerfile pins a non-semver value such as pnpm@latest
`shellcheck -S warning scripts/check-pnpm-pin.sh` exits 0
Running `bash scripts/check-pnpm-pin.sh` against the real repo (post US-001) exits 0

userStories.title: Test the drift-guard script
userStories.description: As the harness developer, I want unit tests for the guard so its behavior is regression-protected in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/__tests__/check-pnpm-pin.test.ts exists, following the harness-config.test.ts convention (vitest + spawnSync, writing both a mock Dockerfile and mock package.json into a temp dir and passing them via --dockerfile/--package-json)
Case A: matching versions -> exit 0
Case B: differing versions -> exit non-zero; output contains both version strings
Case C: Dockerfile missing the corepack prepare pnpm@<ver> line -> exit non-zero
Case D: package.json packageManager has a +sha512.<hash> suffix while Dockerfile pins the bare semver -> exit 0
Case E: Dockerfile still pins pnpm@latest -> exit non-zero (distinct from missing-line case)
At least one case invokes the script from a non-repo-root CWD to prove CWD-independence
`pnpm test:scripts` runs the new test file and all cases pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire the guard into CI and the release validate gate
userStories.description: As the harness developer, I want CI and the release gate to run the guard so drift fails the pipeline rather than reaching a built image.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml boot-lint job gains a step `name: Check pnpm pin drift` running `bash scripts/check-pnpm-pin.sh`, placed after the existing Hadolint step
No separate shellcheck step is added (the existing scripts/*.sh glob already covers the new script)
.github/workflows/release.yml validate job gains the same `Check pnpm pin drift` step (sufficient because release job declares needs: [validate] and the workflow triggers only on tag push)
No existing steps are removed or reordered in either workflow
Both workflow files remain valid YAML (parse-check passes)

userStories.title: Record the change in the changelog
userStories.description: As the harness developer, I want the user-visible reproducibility change recorded so release notes capture it.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md gains an entry under `## [Unreleased]` (### Fixed or ### Changed) describing the devcontainer pnpm pin + drift guard
The entry links issue #114
Keep a Changelog format preserved (entry is one line, imperative mood)
